title: dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. okellyi
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. okellyi, also known as Okelly's orchid, is a subspecies of the spotted orchid (Dactylorhiza fuchsii) native to Ireland. It is a type of terrestrial orchid, which has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, are used to harness the health benefits of Okelly's orchid, including its potential to support respiratory health and reduce fever.
